# Chapter 26 Closing the Audit Period

The audit period cannot be closed until every item has a check mark and is crossed out. When
the day is completed and all the information is correct, the day is closed and, in effect, the
system "freezes" the information. Accurate reports then can be run and balanced to each other
to check for system accuracy. The audit test allows auditors to check that machine meters are
incrementing correctly and communicating to the system properly. Errors and problems can
occur throughout the audit day.




![Figure 26-1 User Tasks List-Close Period Task](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/295.2.png)




## 26.1 Verifying Machine Retirement




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/295.3.png)




Retire machines only after the soft and hard drops are complete. This allows the
retiring machine to make a final drop.

To verify a machine's retirement:

1\. Click Verify Machine Changes in the User Tasks list or Period Timeline. The Verify
Machine Changes task appears.

2\. Click Verify Machine Changes. The Machine Wizard opens.

<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="295" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 26 Closing the Audit Period" -->




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/296.1.png)



3\. Select I want to Verify a machine Add, Retire or Move, then click Next.

4\. Select the machine to retire from the list. If necessary, click one of the following:

· Click Defer to delay completing the selected retirement until the next accounting cycle.
The machine is removed from the verification process and made available during the
next accounting cycle.

· Click Reject to cancel the retirement.

i

Only machines with Yes in the Meter column (meaning their electronic meters have
been received) may be verified.

<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="296" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 26 Closing the Audit Period" -->


<table>
<tr>
<th>Date</th>
<th>Type</th>
<th>Machine</th>
<th>Denom</th>
<th>Location</th>
<th>Meter</th>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>07/09/2017 12:00 AM</td>
<td>Retire</td>
<td>3499</td>
<td>0.01</td>
<td>KVS9901</td>
<td>Yes</td>
</tr>
</table>




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/297.1.png)



5\. Click Next. The Verifying the Retire of Machine window appears.

6\. Select the final meter reading or accept the selection from the Machine Wizard.

7\. Click Next. The Machine Setup Wizard summary window appears.

8\. Review the information, then click OK. The Verify Machine window appears.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/297.2.png)




9\. Click Next. All verifications are completed, and the Maintenance Workbook reappears.
The Next button is not available until all machines have been verified, deferred or
rejected.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/297.3.png)




A check mark for the accounting period appears next to Verify Machine Changes after all
machines are verified, deferred, or rejected.

<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="297" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 26 Closing the Audit Period" -->


## 26.2 Closing the Period

Close the period only after all user tasks are complete.


### To close a period:

1\. Click Close Period in the User Tasks list. The Close Period task appears.

2\. Click Close Period. A confirmation window opens.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/298.1.png)




3\. Click Yes. An information window opens.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/298.2.png)




4\. Click OK. The information window closes, and the period closes.

<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="298" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/299.1.png)
